title: ?field= rejected at from_uri() not put() — intentional
description: wolfhsm stores raw bytes, so ?field= is never valid. The backend rejects it at from_uri() construction time rather than in put() as the CLAUDE.md put() guard pattern requires. This is correct: because no WolfHsmBackend can ever have a field set, there is nothing for put() to guard against. A contributor familiar with other backends (e.g. aws-sm) might add a redundant put() guard. Reject such a PR: the from_uri rejection is the complete and correct guard, and adding a dead put() check is noise.
